scope: AgenC resolves model-owned friendly/pragmatic personality templates and emits the marked developer fragment only for real personality changes.

requiredBehaviors: AgenC renders personality updates as developer-role messages wrapped in the `<personality_spec>` marker pair.
The rendered body preserves the upstream preamble and places the resolved template text after the newline.
edgeCases: Text-part developer messages carrying personality markers are treated as contextual messages during rollback trimming.
The `none` personality never resolves to a developer update message.

requiredBehaviors: The AgenC config/session personality type accepts only `none`, `friendly`, and `pragmatic`.
Built-in default config does not pin a personality before the one-shot migration item runs.
edgeCases: Profile overrides and CLI bootstrap propagation still preserve explicit friendly/pragmatic values.
Turn-context snapshots continue to serialize personality through the rollout event shape.

requiredBehaviors: Model catalog entries may advertise complete personality template variables.
ModelInfo carries the template metadata and support bit into turn construction.
Instruction assembly replaces the personality placeholder and preserves the live AgenC base prompt through the base-instructions placeholder.
edgeCases: Incomplete template variables do not advertise support.
Missing personality leaves the live base prompt unchanged.
`none` strips the personality text rather than producing an update fragment.

requiredBehaviors: Run-turn inserts the personality developer fragment before the current user turn only when a prior turn exists, the current personality differs from the previous personality, the current personality is not `none`, and the model resolves a non-empty template.
Initial turns bake personality into the system prompt when a model template is present instead of duplicating that template as a developer update.
Previous-turn settings persist personality so resume/fork replay can suppress duplicate update messages.
edgeCases: Unchanged personality does not add a fragment.
Models without personality template metadata do not add a fragment.
The stable realtime contextual update ordering remains intact.

requiredBehaviors: The PR-09 goal gate verifies the new personality helper, config enum, model catalog metadata, run-turn wiring, parity artifact, and focused test suite.
edgeCases: The gate rejects the stale generic PR prompt fallback for this item.

intentionalReductions: Remote model metadata ingestion is not part of PR-09; remote template contract suites are reserved for PR-11.
The one-shot pragmatic migration and startup marker handling are reserved for PR-10.
outOfScope: Persistent config migration from legacy personality names.
